ethical_status: Amber acquired pre-2017 disputed; collection provenance unclear
notes: Oldest adult Psephenidae; larvae also in Burmese amber (Succinumanax birmaniasis Bao 2018); no compressional pre-Cretaceous fossil known
Placed in extant subfamily Dexorinae; supports Lycidae crown divergence ≥98.17 Ma; Lycidae fossil record otherwise Eocene (Baltic amber)

ethical_status: Amber acquired before SVP embargo threshold; provenance concerns
notes: Oldest known firefly with light organ; placed in Luciolinae; no pre-Cenomanian lampyrid known; Baltic amber Eocene records are next oldest (~37-47 Ma)
Placed in Thoracophorini (Osoriinae); only known pre-Cenozoic Osoriinae until recently; Yamamoto 2025 described first Eleusinini from same deposit

ethical_status: Amber acquired post-2017 per Legalov 2018 publication date; ethical concern
notes: Contemporaneous with Stephanopachys vetus in Cenomanian Charentes amber (France; ~98 Ma earliest Albian/Cenomanian); Charentes amber is non-Burmese alternative at effectively same age
Oldest Ripidiinae; a Jurassic ripiphorid (unnamed) known from Middle Jurassic China providing older alternative; Albian Spanish amber has oldest Mordellidae (non-Burmese)

fossil_taxon: Paleoendeitoma antennata Deng Slipinski Ren & Pang 2017
ethical_status: Amber acquired pre-2017 disputed; reported from pre-2017 collections
notes: Oldest Zopheridae; multiple additional Burmese taxa (Cretomysteria); Eocene Baltic amber yields Xylolaemus and other colydiinae but ~50 Ma later

Oldest definitive Endomychidae and Coccinelloidea from Burmese amber; subsequent paper (2022 Insects) described first 'Higher Endomychidae' from same deposit; no competing non-Burmese Cretaceous endomychid known

fossil_taxon: Protoliota antennatus Liu Slipinski Wang & Pang 2019
ethical_status: Amber acquired pre-2017 but described 2019; provenance concern
notes: Oldest Silvanidae; second species Protoliota paleus described 2020; Eocene Baltic amber yields Airaphilus Cathartosilvanus Silvanus spp. (~37-47 Ma) as non-Burmese alternative at much younger age
